meddpiccNotes.metrics: Engineering Manager estimated 5 hrs/month saved on license reconciliation.
meddpiccNotes.economicBuyer: Engineering Manager confirmed as sole budget owner for tools under $20k.
meddpiccNotes.decisionCriteria: Simple integration, low lift, no security review needed for this deal size.
meddpiccNotes.decisionProcess: Engineering Manager approves directly; confirmed no further approval needed.
meddpiccNotes.paperProcess: Not applicable — deal size below procurement threshold.
meddpiccNotes.identifyPain: Manual license tracking causing occasional overages.
meddpiccNotes.champion: Engineering Manager was the sole stakeholder throughout, consistently engaged.
meddpiccNotes.competition: None named — no alternative vendor evaluated.

activityTimeline.summary: Product demo, positive reception, no objections.
Proposal sent, verbal agreement to proceed pending internal cost-center approval.
Engineering Manager reports an org-wide Q3 tooling budget cut affecting all non-essential software purchases under $25k; this purchase was cut along with several others on the same list.

emails.subject: Re: License Management Add-on — moving forward?
emails.snippet: Wanted to give you a heads up before this goes stale in the pipeline: we just got hit with an org-wide Q3 tooling budget cut. Every non-essential software purchase under $25k got cut, including this one. Nothing to do with the product — we liked it. Might revisit next fiscal year.

proposal.summary: Verbal agreement to proceed before the budget cut; no pricing or scope objections at any point.
